acceptanceCriteria: Export `truncateWords(text: string, maxWords: number): string` from the text-utilities module.
When text has <= maxWords words, return the text unchanged with no trailing ellipsis.
When text has > maxWords words, return the first maxWords words joined by single spaces, immediately followed by the single character `…` (no leading space).
When maxWords <= 0, return an empty string.
When input is empty or whitespace-only, return an empty string.
Reuse the module's existing word tokenization (normalizeText) — do not introduce a second splitter.
All existing tests for `wordCount` and `estimateReadingTime` continue to pass with zero changes to their implementation or behavior.

New tests for `truncateWords` cover: maxWords=0, negative maxWords, empty/whitespace input, fewer words than max, exactly maxWords, more than maxWords, text with excess internal whitespace, and single-word truncation.
problemStatement: Consumers across the web app need to display hard-capped text previews (cards, list summaries, notification snippets). Without a shared word-based truncation utility, each UI surface must roll its own — leading to inconsistent splitting rules, varied ellipsis handling, and subtle whitespace bugs that erode polish.
userValue: One predictable truncation primitive means designers and developers get the same behavior everywhere: same tokenization as wordCount/estimateReadingTime, same ellipsis, same edge-case handling. This eliminates an entire class of "preview looks wrong" UI defects and reduces boilerplate in every feature that shows abbreviated text.
